summary: American realist novelist, called the "Dean of American Letters." Best known for The Rise of Silas Lapham (paint mogul, Corey family), A Hazard of New Fortunes (magazine Every Other Week, streetcar strike, Basil March), and A Modern Instance (Bartley Hubbard, Marcia Gaylord). Editor of The Atlantic Monthly; championed Chesnutt and Dunbar.

works.name: The Rise of Silas Lapham
works.description: Novel about a paint merchant who marries into the Corey family. Tom Corey courts Penelope.

clues.clue: Title character is a paint mogul — Penelope marries Tom Corey; thwarts sale planned by Milton Rogers
examples: the sisters' father makes a fortune in the paint industry

clues.clue: Tom Corey's relations with sisters Irene and Penelope drive the plot
examples: Tom Corey courts Penelope while a spot in the newspaper series 'Solid Men' is sought

clues.clue: Employs typist Zerilla Dewey; interviewed by Bartley Hubbard
examples: employs Zerilla Dewey as a typist

works.name: A Hazard of New Fortunes
works.description: Novel about the magazine Every Other Week, run by Basil March and bankrolled by Mr. Dryfoos. Conrad dies in a streetcar strike.

clues.clue: Basil March runs magazine Every Other Week, bankrolled by Dryfoos; Conrad dies in streetcar strike
examples: Mr. Dryfoos bankrolls the magazine Every Other Week
Conrad's death in a streetcar strike

clues.clue: Features anarchist Lindau, racist Colonel Woodburn
examples: the anarchist Lindau, the racist Colonel Woodburn

clues.clue: Haymarket affair inspired the climax; title from Shakespeare's King John
examples: The Haymarket affair inspired this novel's climax

works.name: A Modern Instance
works.description: Novel about the declining marriage of Marcia Gaylord and Bartley Hubbard.

clues.clue: Declining marriage of Marcia Gaylord and Bartley Hubbard — love triangle with Ben Halleck
examples: the love triangle between Ben Halleck, Marcia Gaylord, and Bartley Hubbard

clues.clue: "Dean of American Letters" — champion of American Realism
Editor of The Atlantic Monthly — championed Chesnutt and Dunbar
examples: championed Chesnutt's conjure stories and Dunbar's dialect poetry as the editor of the Atlantic Monthly

clues.clue: Criticism and Fiction — contrasted ideal cardboard grasshopper to "simple, natural, and honest" one; essay about "smiling aspects of life"
examples: unfavorably contrasted an ideal grasshopper made out of cardboard

clues.clue: Friendship with Mark Twain; also wrote Indian Summer, A Traveler from Altruria, story "Editha"
examples: wrote about his friendship with Mark Twain in My Mark Twain

comprehensive_summary: William Dean Howells is an American realist novelist known as the "Dean of American Letters." His best-known novel, The Rise of Silas Lapham, follows a paint mogul whose daughter Penelope marries Tom Corey. Silas employs typist Zerilla Dewey and thwarts a sale planned by Milton Rogers. A Hazard of New Fortunes centers on the magazine Every Other Week, run by Basil March and bankrolled by Mr. Dryfoos. The climax features Conrad Dryfoos's death during a streetcar strike inspired by the Haymarket affair. A Modern Instance traces the declining marriage of Marcia Gaylord and Bartley Hubbard. Howells served as editor of The Atlantic Monthly, championing Chesnutt and Dunbar. His Criticism and Fiction discusses the "smiling aspects of life" in American literature.
